sections.paragraph:
εἰκτικῶς adv. of εἰκτικός (fr. εἴκω) ‘readily yielding’ (Philod., Περὶ σημ. 18, 2 Gomp.; Maximus Tyr. 7, 3b) readily yielding 1 Cl 37:2 (s. Bihlmeyer’s note; s. Lightf. app. on the reading of cod. Alexandrinus).—DELG s.v. εἴκω.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 281
